evals.prompt: Build a seasonality curve for my site and tell me when demand peaks and when I should publish.
evals.expected_output: Should aggregate each keyword's 12 monthly volumes (weighted by annual volume) into one site index (mean month = 100), identify peak and trough months plus the peak/trough ratio, and recommend a publish month before each peak using --lead-weeks. Saves a dated JSON report and reports 0 NodesHub tokens.
assertions: Triggers on seasonality / when-do-keywords-peak / best-time-to-publish language
Builds a single normalized site curve (12-month index, mean month = 100)
Identifies peak month(s) and trough month(s) plus the peak/trough ratio
Produces a publishing calendar with concrete 'publish in X -> pays off in Y' lead-time pairs

evals.prompt: Run the seasonality analyzer on the demo data and show me the off-season topics.
evals.expected_output: Should run analyze.py --demo against the built-in summer + counter-seasonal winter fixture, print the 12-month ASCII curve, and surface the winter keywords (snow boots, christmas lights) as counter-seasonal diversification topics that fill the off-season valley.
assertions: Runs with the --demo flag and needs no API key or data file
Prints a 12-month ASCII bar/heatmap of the site index
Site peak lands in summer (around Jul) and trough in spring (around Mar)
Lists counter-seasonal keywords whose peak is opposite the site peak as diversification topics
Each diversification row shows the keyword's own peak month and annual volume
Does not call the NodesHub API

evals.prompt: I have a CSV of monthly search volumes for my keywords. Build the demand calendar with a 8-week publishing lead time.
evals.expected_output: Should ingest the CSV via --volumes (normalizing month columns like jan/january/m1), build the site curve, and recommend publishing roughly two months before each peak (8 weeks). Falls back to a clear DataForSEO setup message if --source dfs is requested without credentials.
assertions: Reads per-keyword monthly volumes from the CSV via --volumes
Normalizes month column names (jan / january / m1 / month_1 all accepted)
Respects --lead-weeks 8 when computing the publish month before each peak
If DataForSEO is requested without DATAFORSEO_LOGIN/PASSWORD, prints a clear setup message and exits gracefully
